# UC-22.2.56 · NIS2 evidence integrity, certification and standards crosswalk control

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Security &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We make sure the proof files you hand auditors still match what the system first produced, and that each legal phrase lines up with the international safety-and-security checklists people expect. When something is missing, renamed, or skipped by the nightly robot, we call it out early.*

---

## Description

Detects evidence packages or scheduled compliance searches whose cryptographic verification failed, whose scheduler runs were skipped, or whose clause metadata cannot be mapped to the ISO/IEC crosswalk lookup.

## Value

Gives assurance teams a single pane to prove that each regulatory clause still chains to a recognised international control—and that the exported proof files were not altered after creation—before an external auditor asks for the mapping spreadsheet.

## Implementation

Emit structured JSON lines into `index=audit_evidence` whenever exports run; include `sha256_expected` from secure storage. Mirror saved-search health from `scheduler` (consider Splunk Monitoring Console). Maintain `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` under GRC change control. Install Splunkbase 1928 if attestation lives in ServiceNow. Route failures to Records & Information owner.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.2.56`, GRC dashboard workbook, ServiceNow task cross-links, and WORM/archival copies in `index=audit_evidence` with hash metadata attached.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

An automated export lands in `audit_evidence` with `hash_verified=false` for `evidence_id=PACK-042` while the crosswalk lookup lacks any row for `nis2_clause=Art.24(placeholder)` so `iso27001_control` stays null; the search returns that evidence_id with scheduler_reason blank.

### Negative scenario

A fresh export arrives with matching `sha256_observed` and `sha256_expected`, `hash_verified=true`, a populated `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` row, and the related saved search completes without `scheduler` skips in the last 24 hours, producing zero rows when the alert runs.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- This UC is a meta-control — it monitors the integrity and completeness of the NIS2 evidence programme itself, rather than monitoring a specific technical or operational control. It covers three NIS2 requirements:
  - **Art.21(2)(a)** — Risk analysis and information-system security policies: the evidence exported by all other UCs must be verifiable (hash integrity) and traceable to recognised standards (crosswalk).
  - **Art.21(5)** — Implementing acts: ENISA's implementing regulation requires entities to use "European and international standards and technical specifications" — this UC proves each NIS2 clause maps to ISO 27001, IEC 62443, and NIST CSF controls.
  - **Art.24/Art.25** — Use of European and national cybersecurity certification schemes and standardisation: entities may demonstrate compliance through certification. This UC tracks whether evidence packages align with certification requirements.
- **Why this matters**: during an audit, the competent authority will ask: "Show me that your evidence has not been tampered with, and show me how each NIS2 requirement maps to your existing ISO 27001 certification." This UC answers both questions. It detects: (1) evidence files whose cryptographic hash has changed since creation (tampering or corruption), (2) NIS2 clauses that have no mapping to recognised standards (crosswalk gaps — indicating either the clause is not covered or the mapping is incomplete), and (3) saved searches that were skipped or deferred (evidence production failures — gaps in the continuous compliance record).
- **Data architecture**: the SPL processes data from two sources:
  - `index=audit_evidence` — the central compliance evidence repository. Every other NIS2 UC writes its results to this index. Each evidence record includes: `evidence_id` (unique package identifier, e.g., "UC-22.2.17-2026-W18"), `nis2_clause` (the NIS2 article this evidence supports, e.g., "Art.21(2)(c)"), `sha256_expected` (the SHA-256 hash computed at creation time and stored securely), `sha256_observed` (the SHA-256 hash recomputed at query time from the stored file), `hash_verified` (true/false — whether `sha256_expected` matches `sha256_observed`), `export_path` (the file path or object URL of the evidence export), `signed_by` (the RFC3161 TSA that countersigned the evidence), `validation_note` (human-readable notes, may contain keywords like "fail", "mismatch", or "expired" indicating problems).
  - `index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler` — Splunk's internal scheduler log. Records every saved search execution with: `savedsearch_name` (the name of the saved search), `status` (success, skipped, deferred, error), `reason` (why the search was skipped/deferred, e.g., "QuotaExceeded", "bundle_push"), `run_time` (execution duration). The UC filters to `savedsearch_name="UC-*"` to focus on NIS2 use case searches only.
- **Standards crosswalk lookup**: `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` with columns: `nis2_clause` (e.g., "Art.21(2)(a)", "Art.21(2)(c)", "Art.23"), `iso27001_control` (the corresponding ISO 27001:2022 control, e.g., "A.5.1 Information security policies"), `iec62443_requirement` (the corresponding IEC 62443 requirement for OT/ICS environments, e.g., "SR 1.1 Human user identification and authentication"), `nist_csf` (the NIST Cybersecurity Framework function/category, e.g., "ID.GV-1"), `enisa_ref` (optional ENISA implementation guidance reference). This CSV is the bridge between NIS2's regulatory language and the international standards that auditors understand. It is maintained by the GRC team under version control (Git with signed tags). Each quarterly update is countersigned with RFC3161.
  Populate from the official mapping resources: the Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) 2024/2690 provides a detailed mapping between NIS2 Art.21(2) measures and ISO 27001/IEC 62443 controls. Validate: `| inputlookup nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv | stats count dc(nis2_clause) as nis2_clauses dc(iso27001_control) as iso_controls dc(iec62443_requirement) as iec_controls`. Expected: `nis2_clauses` ≥ 15 (there are ~15 distinct NIS2 Art.21(2)(a)-(j) + Art.23/24/25 requirements).
- **Evidence integrity mechanism**: the evidence export pipeline (typically a scheduled script or SOAR playbook) computes SHA-256 of each evidence file at creation time and stores it as `sha256_expected` in the evidence metadata record in `index=audit_evidence`. At query time, the pipeline recomputes the hash from the stored file and writes `sha256_observed`. If the two don't match, the file has been modified since creation — either tampered with, corrupted during storage, or replaced. The `hash_verified` field is the boolean comparison result.
- **RBAC**: role `nis2_evidence_manager` with `srchIndexesAllowed = audit_evidence, _internal` and write access to the crosswalk lookup. The `audit_evidence` index must have strict access controls: only the `grc_admins` role should be able to delete events (prevent evidence destruction). Consider implementing `indexes_edit` restrictions to prevent accidental deletion.
- **Index retention**: `index=audit_evidence` ≥ 7 years (NIS2 supervisory proceedings can reference evidence from years prior). `index=_internal` ≥ 30 days (scheduler health).

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
**Evidence metadata ingestion:**
Create a dedicated HEC token for evidence metadata:
- Settings > Data Inputs > HTTP Event Collector > New Token
- Name: `evidence-metadata-hec`
- Allowed Indexes: `audit_evidence` (only)
- Source Type: `evidence:export`
- Restrict by IP: the server running the evidence export pipeline

The evidence export pipeline should POST metadata after each export:
```json
{
  "event": {
    "evidence_id": "UC-22.2.17-2026-W18",
    "nis2_clause": "Art.21(2)(c)",
    "sha256_expected": "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb924...",
    "sha256_observed": "e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb924...",
    "hash_verified": "true",
    "export_path": "s3://compliance-vault/nis2/2026/W18/UC-22.2.17.csv",
    "signed_by": "DigiCert TSA",
    "validation_note": "OK"
  },
  "sourcetype": "evidence:export",
  "index": "audit_evidence"
}
```

Verify:
```
index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export earliest=-30d
| stats count dc(evidence_id) as packages dc(nis2_clause) as clauses_covered values(hash_verified) as hash_statuses
```
Expected: `packages` should grow weekly (each UC exports weekly). `clauses_covered` should match the number of NIS2 UCs actively producing evidence. `hash_statuses` should contain only "true" — any "false" indicates an integrity issue.

**Scheduler health monitoring:**
No additional configuration needed — `index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler` is available by default. Verify:
```
index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler savedsearch_name="UC-*" earliest=-7d
| stats count by status
```
Expected: `status=success` should dominate. `status=skipped` or `status=deferred` should be rare (<1% of total runs). If `status=skipped` is frequent, the search head is overloaded — investigate the Monitoring Console.

**Standards crosswalk lookup:**
Place `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` in `$SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/TA-nis2-evidence/lookups/`. Define in `transforms.conf`:
```ini
[nis2_standards_crosswalk]
filename = nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
```
Example crosswalk rows:
```csv
nis2_clause,iso27001_control,iec62443_requirement,nist_csf,enisa_ref
Art.21(2)(a),A.5.1 Information security policies,SR 1.1,ID.GV-1,IR-2024-2690-S3
Art.21(2)(b),A.16.1 Management of information security incidents,SR 6.2,RS.RP-1,IR-2024-2690-S4
Art.21(2)(c),A.12.3 Information backup,SR 7.6,PR.IP-4,IR-2024-2690-S5
Art.21(2)(d),A.15.1 Information security in supplier relationships,SR 1.13,ID.SC-1,IR-2024-2690-S10
Art.21(2)(e),A.14.2 Security in development and support processes,SR 3.3,PR.IP-2,IR-2024-2690-S11
```

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save as `UC-22.2.56 NIS2 Evidence Integrity and Crosswalk`.

#### Understanding this SPL — design decisions:

- **Dual-source union**: the search combines evidence metadata (`audit_evidence`) and scheduler health (`_internal`) into a single result set. These represent two different failure modes: (a) evidence exists but its integrity is compromised (hash mismatch), and (b) evidence was never produced because the saved search didn't run (scheduler skip). Both are compliance gaps.

- **`hash_verified="false" OR isnull(hash_verified)`**: catches two conditions: (a) the hash was computed and doesn't match (explicit integrity failure — the file has been modified), and (b) the hash was never computed (the export pipeline didn't include integrity checking — an implementation gap). Both need attention.

- **`match(lower(validation_note),"(fail|mismatch|expired)")`**: catches human-annotated problems in the evidence metadata. The validation_note is a free-text field where the export pipeline or manual reviewer can flag issues. Common values: "certificate expired", "hash mismatch on re-verification", "failed integrity check".

- **`status="skipped" OR status="deferred"`**: from the scheduler, catches saved searches that didn't run. "Skipped" means the search was scheduled but couldn't execute (usually due to concurrent search quota exhaustion). "Deferred" means it was pushed to a later time. Both mean evidence was not produced on schedule. The `reason` field often contains "QuotaExceeded" (search head overload) or "bundle_push" (during a knowledge bundle distribution).

- **Crosswalk enforcement**: `lookup nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv nis2_clause OUTPUT iso27001_control, iec62443_requirement` enriches each evidence record with the corresponding ISO/IEC controls. `crosswalk_ok=if(isnotnull(iso27001_control) AND isnotnull(iec62443_requirement),1,0)` checks that both mappings exist. If either is null, the NIS2 clause has no standards mapping — a crosswalk gap that must be addressed before the next audit.

- **`nis2_clause=coalesce(nis2_clause, obligation_clause, control_clause, "Art.21(2)(a)")`**: normalises the clause identifier across different evidence metadata schemas. Some export pipelines use `obligation_clause`, others use `control_clause`. The fallback to `Art.21(2)(a)` ensures that evidence without a clause tag is still processed (and flagged for annotation).

Schedule: cron `0 */2 * * *` (every 2 hours). Evidence integrity should be verified frequently — a tampered evidence file discovered weeks later is much harder to remediate than one caught the same day. Time range: embedded in the SPL (30d for evidence, 7d for scheduler). Trigger: `Number of results > 0`.

Alert actions: (1) For `hash_verified=false`: PagerDuty page to the Records & Information Manager. A hash mismatch is a potential evidence tampering event — treat as a security incident. (2) For crosswalk gaps: email to the GRC team with the unmapped `nis2_clause`. (3) For scheduler skips: email to the Splunk platform team. (4) Write all results to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=nis2:evidence_integrity`.

Extended search — evidence production completeness:
```spl
| inputlookup nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv
| join type=left nis2_clause
    [search index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export earliest=-30d
     | stats latest(_time) as last_evidence dc(evidence_id) as evidence_count by nis2_clause]
| fillnull evidence_count value=0
| eval days_since=if(isnotnull(last_evidence), round((now()-last_evidence)/86400,0), 999)
| eval evidence_status=case(
    evidence_count=0, "NO_EVIDENCE",
    days_since > 30, "STALE",
    1==1, "CURRENT")
| where evidence_status!="CURRENT"
| table nis2_clause iso27001_control evidence_status days_since evidence_count
| sort evidence_status days_since
```
This extended search uses the crosswalk as the driver to identify NIS2 clauses with no recent evidence production. A clause with `evidence_status=NO_EVIDENCE` has never had evidence exported. A clause with `evidence_status=STALE` has evidence older than 30 days. Both are audit findings waiting to happen.

### Step 3 — Validate
(a) **Hash integrity test**: create a test evidence export with a known SHA-256 hash. POST the metadata to HEC with `hash_verified=true` and matching `sha256_expected`/`sha256_observed`. Run the search. Confirm the test record does NOT appear (hash is valid). Now POST a second record for the same `evidence_id` with `hash_verified=false` (simulating a tampered file). Run the search. Confirm the record appears.

(b) **Crosswalk gap test**: add a test row to the evidence metadata with `nis2_clause=Art.99` (a clause that doesn't exist in the crosswalk). Run the search. Confirm the record appears with `crosswalk_ok=0` and null `iso27001_control` / `iec62443_requirement`.

(c) **Scheduler skip detection**: identify a UC saved search in the scheduler log with `status=success`. Temporarily disable the search to cause a "skipped" status on the next run (or use a search with a deliberate quota violation in a dev environment). Run the UC. Confirm the skipped search appears.

(d) **Evidence completeness test**: run the extended search. Verify that all NIS2 clauses in the crosswalk have `evidence_status=CURRENT`. If any show `NO_EVIDENCE` or `STALE`, investigate which UC is responsible for producing evidence for that clause and why it has stopped.

(e) **Crosswalk version control**: verify the crosswalk CSV is under version control: `git log --oneline -5 lookups/nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv`. The last update should be within the current quarter. If the CSV hasn't been updated since the last ISO 27001:2022 revision, it may be stale.

(f) **RFC3161 signature verification**: for a sample evidence file, verify the RFC3161 countersignature is valid. Use: `openssl ts -verify -data <evidence_file> -in <timestamp_token> -CAfile DigiCertTSARootCA.crt`. If verification fails, the timestamp or the evidence file has been modified.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard (recommended name: `NIS2 Evidence Integrity and Standards Compliance`):
- **Row 1 — Evidence health**: Single-value `Hash failures (30d)` (red ≥ 1 — potential tampering). Single-value `Crosswalk gaps` (amber ≥ 1 — unmapped clauses). Single-value `Scheduler skips (7d)` (amber ≥ 5). Single-value `Evidence packages produced (30d)` (count of distinct `evidence_id`).
- **Row 2 — Evidence integrity detail**: table of all evidence records with `hash_verified=false` or crosswalk gaps, showing `evidence_id`, `nis2_clause`, `iso27001_control`, `iec62443_requirement`, `hash_verified`, `export_path`, `last_seen`. Drilldown to the evidence file for investigation.
- **Row 3 — Evidence production matrix**: from the extended search, a table showing every NIS2 clause, its standards mapping, `evidence_status`, `days_since` last evidence, and `evidence_count`. This is the primary artifact for audit preparation — it proves continuous evidence production across all NIS2 requirements.
- **Row 4 — Scheduler health**: `index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler savedsearch_name="UC-*" earliest=-7d | timechart span=1h count by status`. Stacked bar chart showing search execution statuses over time. A spike in "skipped" indicates search head overload — correlate with peak business hours.
- **Row 5 — Crosswalk coverage**: from the crosswalk lookup, a completeness check: `| inputlookup nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv | stats count(eval(isnotnull(iso27001_control))) as iso_mapped count(eval(isnotnull(iec62443_requirement))) as iec_mapped count as total | eval iso_pct=round(100*iso_mapped/total,1) | eval iec_pct=round(100*iec_mapped/total,1)`. Single values for ISO and IEC mapping completeness percentage.

Runbook (owner: CISO / Records & Information Manager):
1. Every 2 hours (automated): investigate alerts.
   - **Hash failure**: this is the most critical finding. A `hash_verified=false` means an evidence file has been modified since creation. Steps: (a) retrieve the original file from backup, (b) compare with the current file to identify what changed, (c) if the change is unauthorised, treat as a security incident, (d) if the change is due to corruption, restore from backup and re-sign with RFC3161, (e) document the incident in the evidence audit trail.
   - **Crosswalk gap**: contact the GRC team to add the missing mapping. This is typically a lookup maintenance issue, not a compliance failure — the control may exist but the mapping hasn't been documented.
   - **Scheduler skip**: contact the Splunk platform team. Common causes: concurrent search quota exhaustion (too many searches running simultaneously), knowledge bundle distribution (during deployments), or search head crash. If skips are frequent, increase the search quota or distribute UCs across multiple search heads.
2. Monthly: review the evidence production matrix. For each NIS2 clause, verify that evidence is being produced on the expected cadence (weekly for operational UCs, monthly for governance UCs, quarterly for strategic UCs).
3. Quarterly: the GRC team reviews the crosswalk CSV. Update for any new ISO 27001/IEC 62443 revisions, new NIS2 implementing acts, or changes to the organisation's certification scope. Git-tag the updated CSV. Archive the previous version with RFC3161.
4. Annually: present the evidence integrity report to the board audit committee. Include: total evidence packages produced, hash failure incidents, crosswalk completeness, and scheduler reliability.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting

- **`hash_verified` is always null.** The evidence export pipeline is not computing hashes. This means every evidence record triggers the `isnull(hash_verified)` condition. Fix: update the export pipeline to compute SHA-256 at creation time. Example in Python: `import hashlib; sha256 = hashlib.sha256(open(filepath, 'rb').read()).hexdigest()`. Include `sha256_expected` and `sha256_observed` (should be identical at creation time) in the HEC payload.

- **Crosswalk shows gaps for every clause.** The `nis2_clause` field in the evidence metadata doesn't match the key format in the crosswalk CSV. The evidence uses full text (e.g., "Article 21(2)(c) Business continuity") while the CSV uses abbreviated format (e.g., "Art.21(2)(c)"). Fix: normalise the `nis2_clause` field in the SPL before the lookup: `eval nis2_clause=replace(nis2_clause, "Article ", "Art.")` and strip descriptive text. Or add alias rows to the crosswalk CSV.

- **Scheduler `status=skipped` during cluster operations.** During search head cluster rolling restarts or captain transfers, saved searches may be skipped as part of normal failover behaviour. Fix: add a filter to exclude brief scheduler blips: `| where NOT (status="skipped" AND match(reason, "captain_transfer|rolling_restart|bundle_push"))`. Alternatively, only alert when skips exceed a threshold: `| stats count(eval(status="skipped")) as skips by savedsearch_name | where skips > 3`.

- **Evidence metadata duplicates.** The export pipeline runs multiple times (e.g., manual re-run after fixing an issue) and produces duplicate `evidence_id` records. The `stats latest() ... by evidence_id` aggregation handles this by keeping only the latest record per `evidence_id`. However, the dashboard's "packages produced" count may be inflated. Fix: add `| dedup evidence_id` in the dashboard panel's base search.

- **`nis2_clause` fallback to Art.21(2)(a) pollutes results.** The `coalesce` defaults untagged evidence to `Art.21(2)(a)`. If many evidence records lack a `nis2_clause` tag, they all map to Art.21(2)(a), inflating its evidence count while leaving other clauses undercovered. Fix: instead of defaulting, flag untagged evidence: `eval nis2_clause=if(isnull(nis2_clause) AND isnull(obligation_clause) AND isnull(control_clause), "UNTAGGED", coalesce(nis2_clause, obligation_clause, control_clause))` and add a dashboard panel showing untagged evidence for remediation.

- **RFC3161 TSA endpoint unreachable during signing.** If the DigiCert TSA endpoint (`http://timestamp.digicert.com`) is unreachable during the evidence export, the countersignature is missing. The evidence file is still valid but not timestamped — its creation time cannot be independently verified. Fix: use a failover TSA (e.g., `http://timestamp.sectigo.com`). Include both primary and failover TSA URLs in the export pipeline configuration. Log TSA failures to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:signing_failure`.


## SPL

```spl
(index=audit_evidence (sourcetype=evidence:saved_search OR sourcetype=evidence:export) earliest=-30d (hash_verified="false" OR isnull(hash_verified) OR match(lower(validation_note),"(fail|mismatch|expired)"))) OR (index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-7d (status="skipped" OR status="deferred" OR like(reason,"%quota%")) savedsearch_name="UC-*")
| eval nis2_clause=coalesce(nis2_clause, obligation_clause, control_clause, "Art.21(2)(a)")
| lookup nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv nis2_clause OUTPUT iso27001_control, iec62443_requirement, nist_csf
| eval crosswalk_ok=if(isnotnull(iso27001_control) AND isnotnull(iec62443_requirement),1,0)
| where crosswalk_ok=0 OR hash_verified="false" OR isnull(hash_verified) OR status IN ("skipped","deferred")
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen latest(reason) as scheduler_reason values(sourcetype) as evidence_channels values(export_path) as paths by evidence_id nis2_clause iso27001_control iec62443_requirement
| sort - last_seen
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Alerts where nodename=Alerts.All_Alerts by Alerts.signature | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table listing evidence_id, nis2_clause, iso27001_control, iec62443_requirement, last_seen; sparkline of scheduler skips; single value for hash failures.

## Known False Positives

• **Manual PDF exports without hashes** — Distinguish: legacy workflow still valid until automation lands. Suppress: time-bound exception register rows tagged `manual_review_ok` with expiry.
• **Scheduler skips during indexer rolling restart** — Distinguish: short **5 minute** blip with `reason` mentioning bundle push. Suppress: filter SPL `where NOT match(reason,"bundle")` or require skips >3 per hour before alerting.
• **Clause aliases from national transposition** — Distinguish: local counsel cites subsection text while lookup expects high-level clause id. Suppress: extend `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` with alias column keyed via secondary lookup `nis2_clause_aliases.csv`.
• **ServiceNow catalog lag** — Distinguish: attestation task closed but REST poll not yet indexed. Suppress: increase Splunkbase 1928 input interval temporarily; join `snow:task` only when `sys_updated_on` < **15 minutes** lag.
• **Pen-test duplicate exports** — Distinguish: same evidence re-emitted hourly during purple-team exercise. Suppress: `dedup evidence_id` plus lookup `nis2_evidence_pen_test_runs.csv` listing approved export windows.
